component.name: dopeIPTV
component.summary: Modern IPTV player for Xtream Codes with EPG

description.p: dopeIPTV is a clean, modern player for your own Xtream Codes or M3U subscription. It offers an embedded mpv player, an electronic programme guide with a timeline grid, movies and series with TMDB metadata, recording and timeshift catch-up, optional Trakt sync, Chromecast support, multiple playlists, themes and translations.
dopeIPTV is a player only: it does not provide any channels or content - you connect it to a service you already subscribe to.

screenshot.caption: Live TV with the electronic programme guide
Themes: five palettes and seven accent colors
The EPG timeline grid: what's on now and next

description.p: Windows joins Linux and macOS, plus timeshift and UI fixes.

ul.li: A portable Windows x64 build (unzip and run, no installer), and a fix for a crash on exit in the windowed build
Channels that advertise catch-up but don't actually serve it now hide the timeshift affordance instead of leaving a dead rewind button and seek bar behind an "archive unavailable" message
The category search magnifier now sits centred in its button

description.p: A UI/UX polish release: a redesigned, responsive left panel and a calmer timeshift player.

ul.li: Redesigned left panel with icons and a collapsible Library group; a layout that never overflows or overlaps at any window size
The timeshift timeline auto-hides after a few idle seconds and reappears on interaction
Accent colours follow your chosen theme everywhere, including the middle-column progress bar and playing highlights

description.p: Timeshift and catch-up TV, a reminders manager, search everywhere, and rock-solid playback.

ul.li: Timeshift and catch-up - a live timeline to scrub back into a channel's archive, DVR-style pause of live TV, and play a specific past programme; the app learns each channel's real archive depth and recovers from over-deep requests
Reminders manager to find and multi-delete programme reminders, plus programme search across the whole guide and a configurable number of upcoming programmes
Search everywhere - category search that also matches the channels inside a category, and left-column search in Favorites, Watch Later, Watched, Recordings and History
Customizable keyboard shortcuts with sensible per-OS defaults
Software decoding is now the default (like standalone mpv) for playback that never glitches; hardware decoding is an opt-in setting with an automatic software fallback
Editable recording title and description, faster channel switching, and fixes for an exit crash, macOS arrow scrubbing and Picture-in-Picture

description.p: Stability and housekeeping: smooth 4K, no bloat over time, and self-healing live TV.

ul.li: Smooth 4K playback - the periodic stutter is fixed (resume-position and caches moved out of the main settings file), and hardware decoding is used across GPUs
No bloat over time - TMDB caches are capped, EPG guides are gzip-compressed on disk, and orphaned guides are cleaned up at startup
Live TV reconnects on drop instead of freezing, with a toggle for single-connection accounts; double-click to play a channel, a last-channel key, and type-a-number to jump
Continue watching for movies and episodes, Recently added, programme reminders, and a discreet once-a-day update indicator

description.p: Keyboard shortcuts, a calmer progress indicator, and playback stability.

ul.li: Keyboard shortcuts throughout - Ctrl+G for the EPG guide, M/P/R/I for mute/PiP/record/stats, arrow keys to zap and nudge volume, and N/Enter in the guide (all documented in the README)
A steadier progress indicator that no longer sticks at "100%" or lingers on screen, with a watchdog to clear it
Playback stability - live streams auto-reconnect after a network hiccup, a stall watchdog recovers a frozen picture, and moving the window no longer drops the stream
Packaging - a fully offline Flathub manifest so the app can ship on Flathub

description.p: A polish release: a collapsible sidebar, custom colours, and fixes.

ul.li: Collapsible sidebar - fold it to a slim icon rail (drag or Ctrl+B), plus a focus mode that hides the list for a player-only layout
Custom colours for the left-column entries, categories, individual items, and favourite folders (a folder's colour applies to all its favourites)
M3U playlists now auto-detect their EPG guide (url-tvg), and gzipped XMLTV guides are read transparently
The EPG grid marks channels with catch-up, flags the channel you're watching, and offers "Play this programme"; click the sidebar logo to jump to what's playing
Fixes: Picture-in-Picture close no longer quits the app, the mini player keeps its size after fullscreen, and the colour picker no longer freezes on some Linux setups

description.p: A polish release: a collapsible sidebar, custom colours, and fixes.

ul.li: Collapsible sidebar - fold it to a slim icon rail (drag or Ctrl+B), plus a focus mode that hides the list for a player-only layout
Custom colours for the left-column entries, categories, individual items, and favourite folders (a folder's colour applies to all its favourites)
M3U playlists now auto-detect their EPG guide (url-tvg), and gzipped XMLTV guides are read transparently
The EPG grid flags the channel you're watching, with a button to jump back to it
Fixes: closing while in Picture-in-Picture no longer quits the app, and the colour picker no longer freezes on some Linux setups

description.p: A big feature release focused on getting started, Trakt and the EPG.

ul.li: One-click "Sign in with Trakt" via the browser - no codes or API keys
Try it with built-in demo channels, or add any free M3U playlist
Movies and episodes auto-mark as watched once you've seen them
A new horizontal EPG timeline grid with timeshift catch-up
Unified favorite folders across channels, movies and series
Adaptive window sizing, an in-app update check, and a recordings size cap

description.p: A large internal reorganisation - no user-visible change, but a much cleaner and safer codebase to build on.

ul.li: The code is grouped into clear layers (providers, core, media, services, ui) with a one-way import rule, so a module can be changed without breaking the layers beneath it
Business logic that did not belong in the window (cover-art resolution, resume positions) is now separate, tested service objects
The Python wheel now ships every subpackage, and the tree passes a clean lint with no dead imports

description.p: Lower memory use, ARM64 Linux builds, and an internal cleanup.

ul.li: Far lower memory use: the EPG index is stored compactly (roughly a quarter of before on big guides) and image caches are capped by size, so idle RAM drops by around a gigabyte with large providers
Faster EPG lookups (binary search instead of scanning), snappier lists on huge channel line-ups
ARM64 Linux builds: an aarch64 AppImage and .deb, for ARM machines (e.g. an ARM Ubuntu VM on Apple Silicon)
Internal restructuring of the main window into focused modules - no user-visible change, but a safer base for new features

description.p: The embedded player is now fully self-contained.

ul.li: Bundle libmpv's own dependencies (ffmpeg, libass, ...), so in-app video works on any machine - not only where a matching mpv/ffmpeg is already installed. Fixes a black or disabled player on, e.g., a fresh Ubuntu 24.04
Never crash if the graphics stack can't create the video surface - the app stays up and offers external playback
CI now verifies the player loads inside a clean container

description.p: Packaging fixes so the app "just works" on more systems.

ul.li: Embedded video now works across distributions - the Linux builds no longer ship their own OpenGL/Mesa stack, which had clashed with the host driver and left a black player on Ubuntu
AppImage runs with no libfuse2 install needed (static runtime)
External mpv/VLC launches correctly from the packaged builds
Flatpak no longer crashes on libmpv builds missing an option
Silenced the fontconfig warning spam on launch

description.p: Bugfix: the embedded player is now included in the packaged builds.

ul.li: Bundle libmpv under the exact soname the player loads, so the in-app video player works in the AppImage/.deb/.dmg (0.4.0 shipped it under the wrong name on Linux)
CI now fails the build if libmpv isn't bundled correctly
Built-in TMDB artwork - posters work out of the box, no account needed
Trakt cross-device watched sync for movies, episodes and whole series
New Watched category split into Local and Trakt
Favourites for movies and series, with a Channels/Movies/Series split and Trakt list sync
Watch Later markers, watched badges, and richer covers throughout
Prompt before recording a different channel than the one playing
